topic: iran us tensions escalate with drone strike and retaliation fears
headline_marked: Iran closes Strait of Hormuz after US strikes; [[at least 2 injured]] in southern city
headline: Iran closes Strait of Hormuz after US strikes; at least 2 injured in southern city
summary: Tehran has announced the closure of the Strait of Hormuz to all shipping after the US launched new strikes on Iranian targets. The move follows retaliatory attacks and has raised international concerns.
details: The United States has carried out new strikes against multiple targets in Iran, following orders from President Donald Trump. In response, Tehran has declared the Strait of Hormuz closed to all ship traffic until further notice.

The Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC) stated that the US repeatedly violated the April ceasefire agreement, leading to the closure of the vital waterway. The IRGC also denied previous US claims of assisting ships through the strait.

The IRGC further claimed to have struck two oil tankers attempting to cross the strait illegally. This escalation follows US military reports of the late-night strikes on Wednesday, June 10, as a response to Iran's 'unreasonable and continued aggression.'

Iranian state media reported explosions in cities near the Strait of Hormuz, including Qeshm Island, Bandar Abbas, and Sirik. In the southern city of Kangan, explosions were reported, resulting in at least two injuries.
caption:
Iran has closed the Strait of Hormuz to all shipping after US strikes. At least two people were injured in explosions in Kangan.

The United States has carried out new strikes against multiple targets in Iran, following orders from President Donald Trump. In response, Tehran has declared the Strait of Hormuz closed to all ship traffic until further notice.

The Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC) stated that the US repeatedly violated the April ceasefire agreement, leading to the closure of the vital waterway. The IRGC also denied previous US claims of assisting ships through the strait.

The IRGC further claimed to have struck two oil tankers attempting to cross the strait illegally. This escalation follows US military reports of the late-night strikes on Wednesday, June 10, as a response to Iran's 'unreasonable and continued aggression.'

Iranian state media reported explosions in cities near the Strait of Hormuz, including Qeshm Island, Bandar Abbas, and Sirik. In the southern city of Kangan, explosions were reported, resulting in at least two injuries.

#Iran #StraitOfHormuz #US #MiddleEast #News

Source: Jamuna TV, Somoy News, Prothom Alo
tweet: Iran closes Strait of Hormuz to all shipping after US strikes on its targets. At least 2 injured in explosions in Kangan. #Iran #US #Hormuz

topic: fifa world cup fever sweeps bangladesh
headline_marked: [[2026 FIFA World Cup]] kicks off today with Mexico vs. South Africa
headline: 2026 FIFA World Cup kicks off today with Mexico vs. South Africa
summary: The quadrennial football tournament begins with an opening ceremony and match at the historic Azteca Stadium. Global stars like Shakira and J Balvin are set to perform.
details: The 2026 FIFA World Cup officially commences today, Thursday, June 11, with a match between Mexico and South Africa at the iconic Azteca Stadium in Mexico City. The opening ceremony will precede the Group A fixture.

Colombian pop star Shakira and Nigerian Afrobeats artist Burna Boy are scheduled to perform the official theme song 'Dai Dai' during the opening ceremony. The event will also feature performances by J Balvin and the pop band Måneskin.

This opening match mirrors the 2010 World Cup, which also featured hosts South Africa against Mexico, ending in a 1-1 draw. This year, Mexico will play South Africa on their home turf at the historic Azteca Stadium.

Mexico's defense is considered a strong point, with experienced goalkeeper Ochoa participating in his record sixth World Cup. The team, a blend of experience and youth, has secured six clean sheets in their last eight matches.
South Africa, on the other hand, is known for its potent counter-attacking style. The African nation has managed to score in each of their last nine World Cup matches.

The World Cup's opening festivities are not confined to Mexico alone. The following day, a second opening ceremony will take place at the BMO Field in Toronto, Canada, featuring performances by Michael Bublé, Alanis Morissette, and Alessia Cara.

The final opening event is scheduled for Saturday, June 13, at 5:30 AM Bangladesh time, at the SoFi Stadium in Los Angeles, USA. This star-studded Hollywood-style gala will include performances by Katy Perry, BLACKPINK's Lisa, Future, and Anitta.
caption:
The wait is over! The 2026 FIFA World Cup begins today with a spectacular opening ceremony and the opening match between Mexico and South Africa.

The 2026 FIFA World Cup officially commences today, Thursday, June 11, with a match between Mexico and South Africa at the iconic Azteca Stadium in Mexico City. The opening ceremony will precede the Group A fixture.

Colombian pop star Shakira and Nigerian Afrobeats artist Burna Boy are scheduled to perform the official theme song 'Dai Dai' during the opening ceremony. The event will also feature performances by J Balvin and the pop band Måneskin.

This opening match mirrors the 2010 World Cup, which also featured hosts South Africa against Mexico, ending in a 1-1 draw. This year, Mexico will play South Africa on their home turf at the historic Azteca Stadium.

Mexico's defense is considered a strong point, with experienced goalkeeper Ochoa participating in his record sixth World Cup. The team, a blend of experience and youth, has secured six clean sheets in their last eight matches.

South Africa, on the other hand, is known for its potent counter-attacking style. The African nation has managed to score in each of their last nine World Cup matches.

The World Cup's opening festivities are not confined to Mexico alone. The following day, a second opening ceremony will take place at the BMO Field in Toronto, Canada, featuring performances by Michael Bublé, Alanis Morissette, and Alessia Cara.

The final opening event is scheduled for Saturday, June 13, at 5:30 AM Bangladesh time, at the SoFi Stadium in Los Angeles, USA. This star-studded Hollywood-style gala will include performances by Katy Perry, BLACKPINK's Lisa, Future, and Anitta.

#FIFAWorldCup #Football #WorldCup2026 #Mexico #SouthAfrica

Source: Jamuna TV, Prothom Alo
tweet: The 2026 FIFA World Cup kicks off today! ⚽️ Join us for the opening ceremony and the first match between Mexico and South Africa at the historic Azteca Stadium. Global stars like Shakira set to perform! #FIFAWorldCup #Football
